name: AWS Health Events Report
description: Monthly review of AWS Health events covering executive summary, critical and high-risk events with operator hints, service/region breakdown, and open follow-ups. Routes to the Ops Excellence domain (health-events leaf agent).

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.prompt:
Generate the executive summary for AWS Health events during {month} {year}.

Compute the explicit calendar bounds for {month} {year} (first day through last day, ISO YYYY-MM-DD). Call `get_health_events` ONCE with `start_date` and `end_date` set to those bounds, with a generous `limit` (e.g. 500) and no other filters.

Treat the `riskLevel` field as authoritative — it is assigned deterministically at ingest. Do NOT re-label events.

Render as markdown:

1. **Headline** — total event count for {month} {year}, plus a one-sentence assessment of overall posture (e.g. "3 CRITICAL events affecting EC2 in us-east-1 dominate the month").
2. **Counts by risk level** — table with columns Risk Level | Count. Order rows: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO. Omit rows with zero count.
3. **Counts by status** — table with columns Status | Count. Cover at minimum `open`, `upcoming`, `closed`.
4. **Counts by event scope** — table with columns Event Scope | Count. Distinguishes account-specific vs public events.

If zero events were returned, render a single line: "No AWS Health events recorded for {month} {year}." and stop.

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Critical & High-Risk Events
sections.prompt:
Generate the critical and high-risk event detail section for {month} {year}.

Compute calendar bounds for {month} {year} (first through last day, YYYY-MM-DD). Call `get_health_events` ONCE with `start_date`, `end_date`, and `risk_level=CRITICAL`. Then call `get_health_events` ONCE with the same dates and `risk_level=HIGH`.

For every returned event, prefer the pre-computed narrative fields:
- `impactSummary` — use as the one-sentence operational summary instead of the raw description.
- `remediationHint` — surface verbatim for any open or upcoming event.
- `affectedResourceTypes` — normalized resource tokens (ec2-instance, rds-cluster, etc.).
Fall back to the raw description if any of these are missing.

Render as markdown:

1. **🔴 Critical events** — for each, render a level-3 heading with the AWS Health service name and the event start time, then four lines:
   - **Status:** `<statusCode>` · **Region:** `<region>` · **Scope:** `<eventScopeCode>`
   - **Impact:** `<impactSummary or description>`
   - **Affected resources:** comma-separated `affectedResourceTypes`, or "not specified" if absent.
   - **Remediation:** `<remediationHint>` if present, otherwise omit this line.
   - **ARN:** `<event ARN>` in inline code.
   If zero CRITICAL events were returned, render: "No CRITICAL events during {month} {year}."

2. **🟠 High-risk events** — same structure as above for HIGH risk events. If zero, render: "No HIGH risk events during {month} {year}."

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Service & Region Breakdown
sections.prompt:
Generate a service and region breakdown of AWS Health events for {month} {year}.

Compute calendar bounds for {month} {year} (first through last day, YYYY-MM-DD). Call `get_health_events` ONCE with those `start_date` and `end_date` and a generous `limit` (e.g. 500).

Aggregate the returned events in-prompt — do NOT make additional tool calls.

Render as markdown:

1. **Top 10 services by event count** — table with columns Rank | Service | Total | CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW. Sort by Total descending. Omit columns whose totals are zero across the table to keep it readable.
2. **Events by region** — table with columns Region | Total | CRITICAL | HIGH. Sort by Total descending. Group `global` (region-less / public events) on its own row labeled "global / cross-region".
3. **Concentration callouts** — one short paragraph naming the single service-region pair with the most events, and noting any service whose CRITICAL count is at least 50% of its total.

If zero events were returned, render: "No AWS Health events recorded for {month} {year}." and stop.

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Open & Upcoming Follow-Ups
sections.prompt:
Generate the open and upcoming follow-up section for {month} {year}.

Compute calendar bounds for {month} {year} (first through last day, YYYY-MM-DD). Call `get_health_events` ONCE with `start_date`, `end_date`, and `status=open`. Then call `get_health_events` ONCE with the same dates and `status=upcoming`.

For every returned event, use `impactSummary`, `remediationHint`, and `affectedResourceTypes` when present.

Render as markdown:

1. **Open events still requiring action** — table with columns Risk | Service | Region | Started | Impact | Remediation. Sort by risk (CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW → INFO), then by start time descending. Truncate Impact and Remediation to ~120 characters each to keep the table readable. If zero open events, render: "No AWS Health events from {month} {year} are still open."

2. **Upcoming scheduled events** — table with columns Risk | Service | Region | Scheduled Start | Scheduled End | Impact. Sort by Scheduled Start ascending. If zero upcoming events, render: "No upcoming scheduled AWS Health events tied to this month."

3. **Operator checklist** — at most 5 bullet items distilled from the open and upcoming lists, each phrased as an imperative action (e.g. "Schedule maintenance window for RDS cluster `arn:...` before 2026-06-15"). Skip the section if there are no open or upcoming events.

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.
